3 min read
1 hour ago

--

Starting my Web Development Internship with Oasis Infobyte was an exciting step in my journey as a developer.
As a Computer Science student, I had already worked on different web development projects, but this internship gave me a chance to work in a more structured way, manage tasks, solve problems, and apply my skills while building a complete project.
For me, this internship was not just about completing a task. I wanted to use it as an opportunity to build something meaningful and improve my practical development skills.
Building Inferno Pizza 🍕
During my internship, I worked on Inferno Pizza, a full-stack pizza ordering website.
When I started the project, my goal was simple: I didn’t want to create just another basic food landing page. I wanted the website to feel like an actual food ordering platform where users could browse pizzas, add items to their cart, place orders, and have a smooth overall experience.
I designed the website around a dark and fire-inspired theme that matched the name Inferno Pizza. At the same time, I focused on keeping the interface modern, responsive, and easy to use.
As the project grew, I worked on features such as user authentication, menu browsing, cart functionality, checkout, and order management.
For the development stack, I used React.js for the frontend, Node.js and Express.js for the backend, and MongoDB for managing the application’s data.
Going Beyond the Basic Requirements 🚀
While working on Inferno Pizza, I decided not to stop with the basic functionality.
I wanted to understand how different parts of a full-stack application work together, so I started adding more features to the project.
One of the major features I worked on was an admin dashboard and inventory management system.
The inventory system manages stock updates, deducts inventory after order confirmation, restores stock when an order is cancelled, handles out-of-stock conditions, and supports low-stock alerts.
I also worked on payment and order workflows, including online payment integration and Cash on Delivery.
Working on these features made the project more challenging, but it also made the development process much more interesting.
The Challenges I Faced
Of course, not everything worked perfectly on the first try.
There were moments when cart values were not updating correctly. Sometimes the order flow needed debugging, and at other times the backend logic behaved differently from what I expected.
A lot of my time went into finding out why something was not working rather than simply writing new code.
And surprisingly, that became one of the most valuable parts of the entire internship.
I learned how to trace the application flow, test APIs, check database updates, debug backend logic, and fix problems step by step.
Every bug I solved helped me understand my own project better.
What I Learned 💻
This internship helped me become more comfortable with technologies such as React.js, Node.js, Express.js, MongoDB, REST APIs, Git, and GitHub.
But beyond technologies, I also learned something equally important: building a complete application requires patience and problem-solving.
Writing code is only one part of development.
Understanding the problem, planning the logic, testing different scenarios, debugging errors, and improving the user experience are also important parts of building a good project.
My Experience with Oasis Infobyte
My internship journey with Oasis Infobyte gave me an opportunity to work independently and apply my development knowledge through practical work.
The internship encouraged me to explore ideas, solve development challenges, and improve my project instead of limiting myself to the most basic implementation.
I am grateful for the opportunity and the learning experience I gained during my OIBSIP internship journey.
This experience has motivated me to continue exploring full-stack development and build more complete, real-world applications.
What’s Next?
Completing this internship is another step in my development journey.
There is still a lot I want to learn, especially in backend development, system design, and building scalable applications.
For now, I am happy with the progress I made while building Inferno Pizza and with everything I learned during this internship.
I’m excited to keep learning, solving problems, and most importantly — keep building. 🚀
Thank you, Oasis Infobyte, for this learning opportunity!
— Kartik Singhal
